extra.reasoning:
The monotonicity property is VIOLATED. The interaction_drop rule has the dominant priority 100 and fires only when (abdominal_pain AND vomiting), driving acuity to self_care (the lowest level). Because this rule requires vomiting=true, flipping vomiting from false to true can activate it and lower acuity.

Counterexample (the witness is the vomiting=true 'after' state): abdominal_pain=true, vomiting=true, pallor=false, leg_swelling=false, confusion=false.

Before (vomiting=false, all else identical): only raise_abdominal_pain (priority 2) matches -> acuity = urgent. (interaction_drop does not match because vomiting is false; pallor/leg_swelling/confusion rules do not match.)

After (vomiting=true): both raise_abdominal_pain (p2 -> urgent) and interaction_drop (p100 -> self_care) match. The highest-priority matching rule is interaction_drop (priority 100), so acuity = self_care.

Acuity drops from urgent to self_care when vomiting is set true, lowering acuity. With the enum order self_care < routine < urgent < emergent, this directly contradicts the requirement that setting vomiting true never lowers acuity. No higher-priority rule overrides interaction_drop in the after state since pallor, leg_swelling, and confusion are all false.
